SensorDataset#

SensorDataset abstraction for handling sensor data in urban water systems.

This module defines the ‘SensorDataset’ class used to handle time-series datasets collected by online sensors in the OpenWaterLab (OWL) ecosystem.

The SensorDataset class extends the Dataset class by providing additional functionalities for gap filling using various algorithms.

Classes#

SensorDataset

Extends Dataset class for time-series sensor data measurements.

class owl.data.sensor_dataset.SensorDataset(data, timedata_column='index', data_type='WWTP', experiment_tag='No tag given', time_unit=None)[source]#

Bases: Dataset

Dataset class for handling online sensor time-series.

This class extends the base Dataset with functionality tailored to continuous or high-frequency sensor measurements commonly encountered in water and wastewater systems. In particular it adds various gap filling algorithms to the base class.

Parameters:

  • data (DataFrame) – Time-indexed sensor data, where rows correspond to timestamps and columns correspond to sensor signals.

  • timedata_column (str) – Column used as the time reference, or "index" if the index represents time.

  • data_type (str) – Type of data represented in the dataset.

  • experiment_tag (str) – Label identifying the dataset or experiment.

  • time_unit (str | None) – Unit of the time axis if it is numeric rather than datetime-like (e.g., "min", "h", "d").

Inherits all attributes from 'Dataset'.

Notes

  • The class is intended for continuous or regularly sampled sensor data.

  • It inherits all core functionality from Dataset and may extend it with sensor-specific validation, filtering, gap filling and resampling utilities.

add_to_filled(column_names)[source]#

Add one or more columns into self.filled, seeding them with the validated (i.e., ‘original’ in meta_valid) values from self.data. All other rows become NaN (to be filled later by imputation methods).

Parameters:

column_names (str | Sequence[str]) – Column(s) to add/seed into self.filled.

Return type:

None

Notes

  • If meta_valid is missing or does not contain a column tag, the entire

column is copied (with a warning). - self.filled is created/reindexed to match self.data.index.

calc_daily_average(column_name, arange=None, *, plot=False)[source]#

Compute daily averages and standard deviations for a column.

The method aggregates data on a daily basis and stores the results internally for further analysis or visualization.

Parameters:

  • column_name (str) – Name of the column to aggregate.

  • arange (Optional[Tuple[object, object]]) – Range of data over which the aggregation is performed. Must be compatible with the index type. If None, the full dataset is used.

  • plot (bool) – If True, generate a plot showing daily mean values with standard deviation as error bars.

Returns:

If plot=True, returns the generated figure and axes. Otherwise, returns None.

Return type:

Optional[Tuple[‘matplotlib.figure.Figure’, ‘matplotlib.axes.Axes’]]

Notes

  • If the index is a DatetimeIndex, data is resampled by calendar day.

  • If the index is numeric, days are defined as integer bins [n, n+1).

  • Results are stored in self.daily_average[column_name] as a DataFrame

with columns ["day", "mean", "std"]. - The dataset itself is not modified.

calc_total_proportional(Q_tot, Q, conc, *, new_name='new', unit='mg/l', filled=False)[source]#

Compute a flow-weighted total concentration.

The method calculates a combined concentration based on multiple contributing flows and their associated concentrations.

Parameters:

  • Q_tot (str) – Name of the column containing the total flow (denominator).

  • Q (Sequence[str]) – Names of the columns representing contributing flows.

  • conc (Sequence[str]) – Names of the columns representing concentrations corresponding to each flow in Q (same order).

  • new_name (str) – Name of the output column to create.

  • unit (str) – Unit string assigned to the new column.

  • filled (bool) – If True, perform the calculation using filled data. Otherwise, use the original dataset.

Return type:

None

Notes

  • The total concentration is computed as:

sum(Q_i * conc_i) / Q_tot. - The result is added as a new column to the selected dataset. - The dataset is modified in place.

check_filling_error(nr_iterations, data_name, filling_function, test_data_range, *, nr_small_gaps=0, max_size_small_gaps=0, nr_large_gaps=0, max_size_large_gaps=0, random_state=None, **options)[source]#

Evaluate filling performance using repeated artificial-gap tests.

The method repeatedly creates artificial gaps, applies a filling method, and computes the average reconstruction error.

Parameters:

  • nr_iterations (int) – Number of repetitions used to estimate the average error.

  • data_name (str) – Name of the column to evaluate.

  • filling_function (str | Callable[..., Any]) – Filling method to apply. Can be the name of a method or a callable.

  • test_data_range (Tuple[object, object]) – Range of data over which the evaluation is performed.

  • nr_small_gaps (int) – Number of small gaps to generate per iteration.

  • max_size_small_gaps (int) – Maximum size of small gaps (in number of samples).

  • nr_large_gaps (int) – Number of large gaps to generate per iteration.

  • max_size_large_gaps (int) – Maximum size of large gaps (in number of samples).

  • random_state (int | None) – Random seed for reproducibility.

  • **options – Additional keyword arguments passed to the filling function.

Return type:

None

Notes

  • Each iteration creates artificial gaps and evaluates reconstruction error.

  • The average error (in percent) is stored in self.filling_error.

  • The dataset itself is not modified.

compare_filling_methods(data_name, method_specs, test_data_range, *, nr_iterations=5, nr_small_gaps=0, max_size_small_gaps=0, nr_large_gaps=0, max_size_large_gaps=0, random_state=None, return_errors=False, plot=False)[source]#

Compare multiple filling methods using artificial-gap tests.

The method evaluates several filling approaches by repeatedly creating artificial gaps, applying each method, and summarizing the resulting reconstruction errors.

Parameters:

  • data_name (str) – Name of the column whose filling performance is evaluated.

  • method_specs (Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]) – Mapping from method labels to specification dictionaries. Each specification must include a "filling_function" entry containing either the name of a filling method or a callable. Additional entries are passed as keyword arguments to the method.

  • test_data_range (Tuple[object, object]) – Range of data over which artificial gaps are created and evaluation is performed.

  • nr_iterations (int) – Number of random trials performed for each method.

  • nr_small_gaps (int) – Number of small gaps to generate per iteration.

  • max_size_small_gaps (int) – Maximum size of small gaps (in number of samples).

  • nr_large_gaps (int) – Number of large gaps to generate per iteration.

  • max_size_large_gaps (int) – Maximum size of large gaps (in number of samples).

  • random_state (int | None) – Seed base for reproducibility across methods and iterations.

  • return_errors (bool) – If True, include per-iteration errors in the returned DataFrame.

  • plot (bool) – If True, generate a bar chart of the average filling error for each method.

Returns:

DataFrame summarizing the performance of each filling method. The table includes mean error, standard deviation, number of successful runs, number of attempts, and success rate. If return_errors=True, the per-iteration error values are also included.

Return type:

DataFrame

Notes

  • If a method specification does not provide to_fill, it is set

automatically to data_name. - Artificial gaps are generated independently for each iteration. - Results are sorted by mean reconstruction error, with lower values indicating better performance. - The dataset itself is not modified.

drop_index_duplicates(*, keep='first', sort_index=True, print_number=True)[source]#

Remove duplicate index entries from the dataset.

The method drops duplicated index rows from the data and applies the same selection to associated metadata and auxiliary data structures.

Parameters:

  • keep (Literal['first', 'last', False]) – Which duplicate entries to retain. If False, all occurrences of duplicated index labels are removed.

  • sort_index (bool) – If True, sort all affected data structures by index after removal.

  • print_number (bool) – If True, print the number of duplicate rows removed.

Return type:

None

Notes

  • The same positional mask is applied consistently across data and

related attributes (e.g., metadata and filled data). - The operation modifies the dataset in place.

fill_missing_arima(to_fill, arange=None, *, order=(1, 0, 1), seasonal_order=None, trend=None, max_gap=None, enforce_stationarity=True, enforce_invertibility=True, plot=False, clear=False, fit_kwargs=None)[source]#

Fill missing values using ARIMA/SARIMA model predictions.

The method fills filtered values in a column using in-sample predictions from a SARIMAX (ARIMA/SARIMA) model, while preserving original observations.

Parameters:

  • to_fill (str) – Name of the column to fill.

  • arange (Tuple[object, object] | None) – Range of data over which filling is applied. If None, the full dataset is considered.

  • order (Tuple[int, int, int]) – Non-seasonal ARIMA order (p, d, q).

  • seasonal_order (Tuple[int, int, int, int] | None) – Seasonal ARIMA order (P, D, Q, s). If None, no seasonal component is used.

  • trend (Literal['n', 'c', 't', 'ct'] | None) – Trend specification for the SARIMAX model.

  • max_gap (int | None) – Maximum length of consecutive filtered values to fill. If None, no restriction is applied.

  • enforce_stationarity (bool) – Whether to enforce stationarity in the ARIMA model.

  • enforce_invertibility (bool) – Whether to enforce invertibility in the ARIMA model.

  • plot (bool) – If True, generate a diagnostic plot after filling.

  • clear (bool) – If True, reset previous filling results before applying the method.

  • fit_kwargs (dict | None) – Additional keyword arguments passed to the model fitting routine.

Return type:

None

Notes

  • Only values currently tagged as "filtered" are considered for filling.

  • Original values are preserved.

  • Predictions are generated using a SARIMAX model fitted on the selected data.

  • Filled values are written to self.filled[to_fill].

  • Metadata is updated in self.meta_filled[to_fill] with the tag

"filled_arima" where filling is successful. - If max_gap is provided, only filtered runs with length less than or equal to max_gap are filled. - The operation modifies the dataset in place.

fill_missing_daybefore(to_fill, arange, range_to_replace=[1.0, 4.0], *, only_checked=True, plot=False, clear=False)[source]#

Fill missing values using data from the previous day.

The method fills filtered values in a column by copying values from the same time-of-day on the previous day, using the best available source.

Parameters:

  • to_fill (str) – Name of the column to fill.

  • arange (Tuple[object, object]) – Range of data over which filling is applied. The range must begin at least one day after the start of the dataset.

  • range_to_replace (List[float]) – Minimum and maximum consecutive gap length, expressed in days, that will be filled. These values are converted to numbers of samples based on the previous-day sample count.

  • only_checked (bool) – If True, restrict filling to indices tagged as "filtered". Otherwise, consider all rows in arange.

  • plot (bool) – If True, generate a diagnostic plot after filling.

  • clear (bool) – If True, reset previous filling results before applying the method.

Return type:

None

Notes

  • The method requires equidistant sampling.

  • Values are copied from the same time-of-day on the previous day.

  • When available, values from self.filled[to_fill] are used first;

otherwise, values from self.data[to_fill] are used. - Original values are preserved. - Filled values are written to self.filled[to_fill]. - Metadata is updated in self.meta_filled[to_fill] to reflect filled values. - The dataset is modified in place.

fill_missing_gaussian(to_fill, arange=None, *, only_checked=True, clear=False, plot=False, max_train_points=5000, stride=1, kernel=None, seasonal_period=None, unit='d', alpha=None, normalize_y=True, n_restarts_optimizer=2, random_state=None, context_expand=None, X_cols=None)[source]#

Fill missing values using Gaussian Process regression.

The method fits a Gaussian Process (GP) model on observed data and predicts values at filtered indices, optionally incorporating exogenous variables.

Parameters:

  • to_fill (str) – Name of the column to fill.

  • arange (Tuple[object, object] | None) – Range of data over which the model is trained and applied. If None, the full dataset is used.

  • only_checked (bool) – If True, restrict predictions to indices marked as checked/valid for filling.

  • clear (bool) – If True, reset previous filling results before applying the method.

  • plot (bool) – If True, generate a diagnostic plot after filling.

  • max_train_points (int) – Maximum number of training points used for GP fitting.

  • stride (int) – Subsampling step applied to training data to reduce computational cost.

  • kernel (object | None) – Custom kernel for the Gaussian Process model.

  • seasonal_period (Timedelta | float | None) – Period of a seasonal component to include via a periodic kernel.

  • unit (str) – Unit used when interpreting numeric time values.

  • alpha (float | None) – Value added to the diagonal of the kernel matrix for numerical stability.

  • normalize_y (bool) – Whether to normalize the target variable before fitting.

  • n_restarts_optimizer (int) – Number of restarts for kernel hyperparameter optimization.

  • random_state (int | None) – Random seed for reproducibility.

  • context_expand (Timedelta | None) – Optional extension of the training window around arange.

  • X_cols (Sequence[str] | None) – Additional columns used as exogenous input features.

Return type:

None

Notes

  • The model is trained on points tagged as "original" in meta_valid.

  • Predictions are made for indices tagged as "filtered".

  • Filled values are written to self.filled[to_fill].

  • Metadata is updated in self.meta_filled[to_fill] with the tag

"filled_gaussian" where filling is successful. - Gaussian Process training scales cubically with the number of points; use max_train_points and stride to control computational cost. - A periodic kernel can be included via seasonal_period to capture repeating patterns. - The operation modifies the dataset in place.

fill_missing_interpolation(to_fill, range_, arange=None, *, method='time', limit_direction='both', plot=False, clear=False, **kwargs)[source]#

Fill short missing segments using interpolation.

The method fills filtered values in a column by interpolating over short gaps, while preserving original values and longer missing segments.

Parameters:

  • to_fill (str) – Name of the column to fill.

  • range (int) – Maximum length of consecutive filtered values to be filled. Longer segments remain unfilled.

  • arange (Tuple[object, object] | None) – Range of data over which filling is applied. If None, the full dataset is considered.

  • method (str) – Interpolation method passed to the underlying interpolation routine.

  • limit_direction (str) – Direction for interpolation (e.g., "forward", "backward", "both").

  • plot (bool) – If True, generate a diagnostic plot of the filling results.

  • clear (bool) – If True, reset previous filling results before applying the method.

  • **kwargs – Additional keyword arguments passed to the interpolation function.

Return type:

None

Notes

  • Only filtered values within segments of length <= range_ are filled.

  • Non-filtered values remain unchanged.

  • Filled values are stored in self.filled[to_fill].

  • Metadata is updated in self.meta_filled[to_fill] with the tag

"filled_interpol" for successfully filled points. - Longer filtered segments remain unfilled and retain their original tags. - The operation modifies the dataset in place.

fill_missing_kalman(to_fill, arange=None, *, model='local_level', seasonal_periods=None, max_gap=None, plot=False, clear=False, fit_kwargs=None)[source]#

Fill missing values using a state-space Kalman smoother.

The method fills filtered values in a column using estimates from a structural time-series model, while preserving original observations.

Parameters:

  • to_fill (str) – Name of the column to fill.

  • arange (Tuple[object, object] | None) – Range of data over which filling is applied. If None, the full dataset is considered.

  • model (Literal['local_level', 'local_linear_trend']) – Type of structural model used for smoothing.

  • seasonal_periods (int | None) – Length of a deterministic seasonal component, expressed in number of samples.

  • max_gap (int | None) – Maximum length of consecutive filtered values to fill. If None, no restriction is applied.

  • plot (bool) – If True, generate a diagnostic plot after filling.

  • clear (bool) – If True, reset previous filling results before applying the method.

  • fit_kwargs (dict | None) – Additional keyword arguments passed to the model fitting routine.

Return type:

None

Notes

  • Only values currently tagged as "filtered" are considered for filling.

  • Original values are preserved.

  • Filled values are written to self.filled[to_fill].

  • Metadata is updated in self.meta_filled[to_fill] with the tag

"filled_kalman" where filling is successful. - If max_gap is provided, only filtered runs with length less than or equal to max_gap are filled. - The operation modifies the dataset in place.

fill_missing_model(to_fill, to_use, arange=None, *, only_checked=True, unit='d', plot=False, clear=False, tolerance=None)[source]#

Fill missing values using an external model time series.

The method fills filtered values in a column using values from an external series or model output, aligned to the dataset index.

Parameters:

  • to_fill (str) – Name of the column to fill.

  • to_use (Series | DataFrame) – External time series providing values for filling.

  • arange (Tuple[object, object] | None) – Range of data over which filling is applied. If None, the full dataset is considered.

  • only_checked (bool) – If True, restrict filling to indices tagged as "filtered".

  • unit (str) – Unit used when interpreting numeric time values for alignment.

  • plot (bool) – If True, generate a diagnostic plot after filling.

  • clear (bool) – If True, reset previous filling results before applying the method.

  • tolerance (Timedelta | float | None) – Maximum allowed difference when aligning external data to the dataset index.

Return type:

None

Notes

  • External data is aligned to the dataset index before filling.

  • Only values currently tagged as "filtered" are filled (unless

only_checked=False). - Original values are preserved. - Filled values are written to self.filled[to_fill]. - Metadata is updated in self.meta_filled[to_fill] to reflect filled values. - The dataset is modified in place.

fill_missing_ratio(to_fill, to_use, ratio=None, arange=None, *, intercept=0.0, estimate_params=False, train_range=None, train_only_original=True, zero_intercept=False, robust=False, min_train_points=20, only_checked=True, plot=False, clear=False, return_params=False)[source]#

Fill missing values using a linear relationship with another column.

The method fills filtered values in a column using a linear relation of the form new = ratio * data[to_use] + intercept, with optional parameter estimation.

Parameters:

  • to_fill (str) – Name of the column to fill.

  • to_use (str) – Name of the reference (driver) column.

  • ratio (float | None) – Multiplicative factor. Required if estimate_params=False.

  • arange (Tuple[object, object] | None) – Range of data over which filling is applied. If None, the full dataset is considered.

  • intercept (float) – Additive constant applied in the linear relation.

  • estimate_params (bool) – If True, estimate ratio and intercept from data.

  • train_range (Tuple[object, object] | None) – Range used to estimate parameters. Defaults to arange if provided, otherwise the full dataset.

  • train_only_original (bool) – If True, use only points tagged as "original" for training.

  • zero_intercept (bool) – If True, force the intercept to zero during estimation.

  • robust (bool) – If True, use a robust regression method (e.g., RANSAC) if available.

  • min_train_points (int) – Minimum number of samples required to estimate parameters.

  • only_checked (bool) – If True, restrict filling to indices tagged as "filtered".

  • plot (bool) – If True, generate a diagnostic plot after filling.

  • clear (bool) – If True, reset previous filling results before applying the method.

  • return_params (bool) – If True, return the fitted or used parameters.

Returns:

If return_params=True, returns a dictionary with keys {"ratio", "intercept"}. Otherwise, returns None.

Return type:

Dict[str, float] | None

Notes

  • If estimate_params=True, parameters are fitted using the specified

training range and constraints. - Only values currently tagged as "filtered" are filled (unless only_checked=False). - Filled values are written to self.filled[to_fill]. - Metadata is updated in self.meta_filled[to_fill] to reflect filled values. - The dataset is modified in place.

fill_missing_standard(to_fill, arange=None, *, only_checked=True, plot=False, clear=False)[source]#

Fill missing values using a standard daily profile.

The method fills filtered values in a column using the average daily profile computed from the data.

Parameters:

  • to_fill (str) – Name of the column to fill.

  • arange (Tuple[object, object] | None) – Range of data over which filling is applied. If None, the full dataset is considered.

  • only_checked (bool) – If True, restrict filling to indices tagged as "filtered".

  • plot (bool) – If True, generate a diagnostic plot after filling.

  • clear (bool) – If True, reset previous filling results before applying the method.

Return type:

None

Notes

  • Filling is based on the average daily profile computed by

calc_daily_profile(). - Values are matched by time-of-day. - Original values are preserved. - Filled values are written to self.filled[to_fill]. - Metadata is updated in self.meta_filled[to_fill] with the tag "filled_average_profile" where filling is successful. - The dataset is modified in place.
